title: Dubyaea bhotanica
taxonomy: Dubyaea bhotanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Dubyaea, which is a group of plants that are often found in mountainous regions. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many well-known species. Dubyaea bhotanica is native to the Himalayan region and is often found in high-altitude areas with rocky soil. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships between Dubyaea bhotanica and other plants in the same family.
uses: Dubyaea bhotanica, a plant native to the Himalayan region, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever and alleviating various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare medicinal concoctions that are said to treat digestive issues and boost the immune system. Dubyaea bhotanica is also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Dubyaea bhotanica as a medicinal plant.

intro: Dubyaea bhotanica, also known as the Bhotan Dubyaea, is a perennial plant species native to the Eastern Himalayas, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dubyaea bhotanica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, coughs, and sore throats.
